segments.text: These are the judgments which thou shalt set before them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve: and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If he came in by himself, he shall go out by himself: if he were married, then his wife shall go out with him.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: by himself: Heb. with his body

segments.text: If his master have given him a wife, and she have born him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master's, and he shall go out by himself.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free.
segments.speaker: Hebrew Servant

segments.text: Then his master shall bring him unto the judges; he shall also bring him to the door, or unto the door post;
and his master shall bore his ear through with an aul; and he shall serve him for ever.
And if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If she please not her master, who hath betrothed her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed: to sell her unto a strange nation he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: please…: Heb. be evil in the eyes of, etc

segments.text: And if he have betrothed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If he take him another wife; her food, her raiment, and her duty of marriage, shall he not diminish.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out free without money.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall be surely put to death.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver him into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall surely be put to death.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if men strive together, and one smite another with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keepeth his bed:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit: only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if a man smite his servant, or his maid, with a rod, and he die under his hand; he shall be surely punished.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Notwithstanding, if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished: for he is his money.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If men strive, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart from her, and yet no mischief follow: he shall be surely punished, according as the woman's husband will lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if any mischief follow, then thou shalt give life for life,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if a man smite the eye of his servant, or the eye of his maid, that it perish; he shall let him go free for his eye's sake.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if he smite out his manservant's tooth, or his maidservant's tooth; he shall let him go free for his tooth's sake.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die: then the ox shall be surely stoned, and his flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But if the ox were wont to push with his horn in time past, and it hath been testified to his owner, and he hath not kept him in, but that he hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and his owner also shall be put to death.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Whether he have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If the ox shall push a manservant or a maidservant; he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit, and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The owner of the pit shall make it good, and give money unto the owner of them; and the dead beast shall be his.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if one man's ox hurt another's, that he die; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money of it; and the dead ox also they shall divide.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Or if it be known that the ox hath used to push in time past, and his owner hath not kept him in; he shall surely pay ox for ox; and the dead shall be his own.
segments.speaker: God
